claim: Routines in Claude Code require API keys to be stored as environment variables, not in .env files, because GitHub repos clone without .gitignore-d files
actionable: When setting up remote Claude Code routines, explicitly configure API keys in cloud environment variables—do not rely on .env files which won't be available in remote sessions.
source_title: How to Build 24/7 Claude Agents. Easy.

claim: Remote Claude Code routines cannot access local state like browser cookies or session data—each run is stateless and the GitHub clone is deleted after execution
actionable: Do not use browser automation with stored cookies in remote routines; instead authenticate via API keys, headers, or endpoints that don't require session state.
source_title: How to Build 24/7 Claude Agents. Easy.

claim: Writing routines as specific one-shot prompts (not interactive) is critical because they must succeed without asking clarifying questions
actionable: Craft routine prompts to be explicit and self-contained—avoid any prompt that requires back-and-forth clarification or human intervention to execute.
source_title: How to Build 24/7 Claude Agents. Easy.

claim: Routines minimum interval is 1 hour; Pro plan allows 5 daily runs, Max plan allows 15 daily runs
actionable: Plan your routine frequency around your subscription tier—Pro users get 5 daily routine runs, Max users get 15, with a 1-hour minimum interval.
source_title: How to Build 24/7 Claude Agents. Easy.

claim: Setting 'trusted' network access blocks outbound requests to unverified domains; 'full' access allows any outbound request but risks malicious content exfiltration
actionable: For private repos with controlled inputs, 'trusted' network access is usually sufficient and safer; only use 'full' when you need access to external services and understand the risk.
source_title: How to Build 24/7 Claude Agents. Easy.
